---
title: "Publish and preview"
description: "How editing, previewing, and publishing your menu works. Diners only see what you publish."
sidebarTitle: "Publish and preview"
icon: "eye"
---

Rumizi separates **working copy** (what you're editing) from **published
copy** (what diners see). Every change you make is invisible to the public
until you publish it.

## Working vs published

<Columns cols={2}>
  <Card title="Working copy" icon="pen">
    The live editor state — items, categories, modifiers, photos. Only
    you and your staff see it. Edit freely; nothing leaks to diners.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Published copy" icon="globe">
    An immutable snapshot of the menu that diners actually scan. You
    create it by pressing **Publish**.
  </Card>
</Columns>

## Publishing

<Steps>
  <Step title="Finish your edits">
    Get everything in the editor the way you want it.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Tap Publish">
    From the menu editor header, tap **Publish**. Rumizi takes a
    snapshot of the current menu and marks that snapshot as the live one.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Diners see it instantly">
    The next scan of your QR code shows the new menu. Nothing else to do.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Tip>
The **Menus** list in your workspace shows "Unpublished changes" next to
any menu where the working copy is ahead of the published copy.
</Tip>

## Preview before publishing

If you want to see how a change looks to a diner without publishing it:

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open your public menu URL">
    Go to `rumizi.com/your-venue`.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Add the preview flag">
    Append `?menu=<menu-id>` to the URL. When you're signed in as owner or
    staff, that URL shows the **working** copy instead of the published
    snapshot.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Check it, then publish">
    Happy with the preview? Go back to the editor and publish.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Revert the last publish

Pushed something by accident? From the menu editor header, pick **Revert
last publish**. The pointer moves back to the previous published snapshot.
Diners see the previous version immediately.

## A few things that don't need a publish

Some changes are live immediately, because they're about **availability**
rather than **content**:

- Marking an item as **Out of stock** (or back in stock).
- Toggling **Featured** on an item (adds or removes it from the Popular
  row).

Those take effect on the next page load without any publishing.

## Multiple menus

If you have several menus (for example *Lunch* and *Dinner*), each one
publishes independently. The menu that appears to diners first is
controlled by [open hours](/vendors/menu/scheduling) and the order you set
in the **Menus** list.
